## Pull request overview This PR significantly expands Reggie’s runtime and codegen capabilities to better handle large, capture-heavy (Grok-style) patterns with improved compatibility, lower-allocation capture extraction, and new deterministic “linear token sequence” execution routing. **Changes:** - Adds runtime API enhancements: `matchInto` / `findMatchInto`, `CapturePolicy` + `ReggieOptions`, and a public `UnsupportedPatternException` for precise unsupported-pattern handling. - Improves parser compatibility and routing: atomic groups `(?>...)` (parsed as non-capturing), `\Q...\E` quoted literals (including inside char classes), plus expanded tests/resources for real Grok patterns. - Introduces scalability and correctness work across DFA/NFA/codegen: DFA state-budget handling + DFA_TABLE backend, structural-hash hardening, multiple anchor/quantifier/backref correctness fixes, and new fuzzing infrastructure. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 89 out of 89 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

## Pull request overview This PR fixes two remaining PCRE conformance issues around capturing group extraction when (1) lookaheads appear inside capturing groups and (2) capturing groups end with an optional greedy quantifier that must backtrack to allow subsequent tokens to match. **Changes:** - Add analysis-time detection for lookahead-inside-capturing-group and route those patterns away from the DFA strategy to an NFA-with-lookaround strategy. - Extend greedy-group overlap analysis to recognize trailing optional quantifiers inside ConcatNode children, and extend recursive-descent bytecode generation to do two-path backtracking for such groups. - Add targeted runtime and analyzer tests covering both PCRE failures. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | reggie-runtime/src/test/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/runtime/LookaheadGroupCaptureTest.java | Adds a regression test for lookahead nested in alternation inside a capturing group. | | reggie-runtime/src/test/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/runtime/GreedyOptionalBacktrackTest.java | Adds a regression test ensuring an optional greedy token inside a capturing group backtracks when required by the suffix. | | reggie-codegen/src/test/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/codegen/analysis/PatternAnalyzerGreedyGroupCharSetTest.java | Adds analyzer-level tests verifying strategy selection for trailing optional quantifiers that overlap (or don’t) with the next element. | | reggie-codegen/src/main/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/codegen/codegen/RecursiveDescentBytecodeGenerator.java | Adds detection and two-path backtracking for capturing groups with trailing optional greedy quantifiers. | | reggie-codegen/src/main/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/codegen/analysis/PatternAnalyzer.java | Adds lookahead-inside-capturing-group detection and enhances greedy-group charset extraction for concat-ending optionals. |

comments.author: copilot-swe-agent
comments.body:
> @Copilot review
> 

Reviewed the current state of the PR (latest commit `036b27b`). All three previously-raised issues have been addressed:

1. **`hasLookaheadInsideCapturingGroup` guard** — the `!ignoreGroupCount` gate has been removed so lookahead-in-capturing-group patterns are routed to `OPTIMIZED_NFA_WITH_LOOKAROUND` regardless of whether the call comes from hybrid mode.

2. **Trailing-optional detection tightened to `max == 1`** — `getGreedyGroupCharSet` now only treats `?` (`{0,1}`) as a trailing optional, excluding broader `{0,n}` bounds that the two-path backtracking implementation doesn't handle.

3. **`generateRemainingWithBacktracking` extended** — `hasTrailingOptionalQuantifier` is now checked alongside `extractTrailingOptionalBackref` in the "next backtracking group" search, so two-path backtracking is applied consistently across all remaining concat children.

CI is passing (green) on the latest commit. No additional issues found.

## Pull request overview Fixes a DFA-with-assertions correctness bug where lookbehind assertions were re-evaluated incorrectly after unbounded quantifiers, and adjusts bytecode-gen ordering so failed assertions don’t advance the recorded match end. Also updates fallback detection/tests and introduces a new public `com.datadoghq.reggie.ReggieMatcher` base type. **Changes:** - Attach assertion checks to the assertion NFA state itself during subset construction (prevents assertion re-firing through loops). - Reorder greedy-scan bytecode to evaluate assertions before updating `longestMatchEnd` / `lastAcceptingPos`, and add missing greedy lookahead charset handling. - Remove the “lookbehind + unbounded quantifier” fallback guard and add regression/acceptance tests; add new public matcher base class. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

comments.body:
### 💡 Codex Review https://github.com/DataDog/java-reggie/blob/b7ee87679951f5fafa2e40b283b7d28bbf12bfe8/reggie-codegen/src/main/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/codegen/analysis/FallbackPatternDetector.java#L62 **![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat) Keep fallback for switch-sized lookbehind quantifiers** When this guard is removed, patterns such as `(?<=\d)[a-z]{20,}` no longer fall back, but `PatternAnalyzer` routes assertion DFAs with 20+ states to `DFA_SWITCH_WITH_ASSERTIONS` and `DFASwitchBytecodeGenerator` checks assertions only after consuming the current character (`posVar` is already incremented). For a lookbehind attached to the start state, that makes the switch path look behind the first matched letter rather than the match start, so valid inputs like `3abcdefghijklmnopqrst` are rejected. Please keep the fallback for switch-sized cases or fix the switch generator’s assertion timing before allowing these through.

## Pull request overview This PR fixes incorrect handling of alternation inside fixed-width lookbehind assertions (issue #30) by correcting assertion-specific optimizations and structural hashing/caching, and by removing the now-unnecessary fallback-to-`java.util.regex` guard for this pattern class. **Changes:** - Fixes `NFABytecodeGenerator` lookbehind assertion optimizations so alternations don’t collapse to the first epsilon branch, and charsets are accumulated across alternatives. - Fixes structural hashing to distinguish positive vs negative assertions to prevent incorrect STRUCTURE_CACHE reuse. - Updates fallback detection and adds/updates runtime + processor tests to validate native handling and regressions. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

## Pull request overview Fixes incorrect native-engine behavior for patterns that reference the same capturing group multiple times (e.g. `(\w+)\s+\1\s+\1`), removing the previous blanket fallback to `java.util.regex` and restoring build-time/native compilation for those patterns. **Changes:** - Add runtime acceptance tests covering triple/quadruple backrefs and various separators/anchors. - Adjust strategy selection to avoid unsafe `indexOf`-based start-position skipping for `OPTIMIZED_NFA_WITH_BACKREFS`. - Fix NFA required-literal extraction to stop stitching literals across backreference states; remove the multi-backref fallback guard. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | reggie-runtime/src/test/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/runtime/MultiBackrefCorrectnessTest.java | Adds acceptance tests for multiple backreferences to the same group. | | reggie-runtime/src/test/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/runtime/FallbackVerificationTest.java | Updates “Bug 1” verification to reflect native handling (no forced fallback). | | reggie-processor/src/test/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/processor/ReggieMatcherBytecodeGeneratorTest.java | Updates build-time rejection tests to stop rejecting multi-backref patterns. | | reggie-codegen/src/main/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/codegen/codegen/NFABytecodeGenerator.java | Prevents required-literal extraction from crossing backref states via epsilon transitions. | | reggie-codegen/src/main/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/codegen/analysis/PatternAnalyzer.java | Disables required-literal `indexOf` optimization for NFA-with-backrefs; adds extra multi-backref detection for variable-capture strategy. | | reggie-codegen/src/main/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/codegen/analysis/FallbackPatternDetector.java | Removes the prior multi-backref fallback rule. |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **reggie-processor/src/test/java/com/datadoghq/reggie/processor/ReggieMatcherBytecodeGeneratorTest.java:474** * This test no longer asserts that multi-backreference patterns are rejected, but it also doesn’t add a positive assertion that bytecode generation now succeeds for a multi-backref pattern (e.g. `(\\w+)\\s+\\1\\s+\\1`). Adding such a “should not throw / produces loadable matcher” case would better lock in the new build-time compilation behavior. ``` @Test void testFallbackPatternsRejected() { // Patterns with known engine bugs must fail at build time, not produce buggy bytecode. // Note: multiple-backref patterns (e.g. (\w+)\s+\1\s+\1) are now handled natively // and no longer rejected. assertThrows( UnsupportedOperationException.class, () -> new ReggieMatcherBytecodeGenerator("test.generated", "AltLookbehind", "(?<=a|b)c") .generate()); } ```

The new `@Override` delegates to `!nameToIndex.isEmpty()`, which covers all three construction paths:
1. 4-arg constructor (no names) → `emptyMap` → `false` ✓

**`NamedMatchResultImpl`** — harmless redundancy:
